title: CISA Alert: Threat Actors Exploit LLM APIs to Automate Phishing Campaigns at Scale
text: CISA and FBI have observed threat actors leveraging large language model (LLM) APIs to automate the generation of highly personalized spear-phishing emails at unprecedented scale. Campaigns attributed to multiple nation-state actors produced over 50,000 unique phishing emails per day targeting critical infrastructure personnel. The emails demonstrated contextual awareness of targets' professional roles, recent public statements, and organizational affiliations. Defense: organizations should deploy email authentication (DMARC, DKIM), train staff on AI-generated content indicators, and consider behavioral email anomaly detection.

title: Indirect Prompt Injection Attacks Against Autonomous LLM Agents: A Systematic Study
text: We present a systematic study of indirect prompt injection attacks against autonomous LLM-based agents with tool-use capabilities. We evaluated 12 state-of-the-art agent frameworks including LangChain, AutoGPT, and Claude-based agents. Our results show that 92% of tested agents are vulnerable to prompt injection via tool outputs. Attackers can exfiltrate user data, escalate privileges, and pivot to connected systems. We introduce a taxonomy of 6 injection vectors: document retrieval, web browsing, code execution, API responses, memory recall, and sub-agent outputs. Proposed mitigations include instruction hierarchy enforcement and output sandboxing.

title: Microsoft Security Report: Adversarial ML Attacks Against AI-Powered Security Products
text: Microsoft Threat Intelligence Center (MSTIC) has documented a 340% increase in adversarial machine learning attacks targeting AI-powered security tools over the past 12 months. Attackers are using model evasion techniques to bypass AI-based malware classifiers, phishing detection systems, and anomaly detection tools. Key attack patterns include adversarial input crafting against transformer-based classifiers, data poisoning of threat detection models through compromised telemetry feeds, and model extraction attacks against vendor-hosted detection APIs. 78% of observed attacks targeted financial services and critical infrastructure sectors.

title: CVE-2025-23456: Remote Code Execution via Deserialization in LangChain Agent Framework
text: A critical deserialization vulnerability (CVSS 9.8) in LangChain versions 0.1.0–0.2.15 allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via crafted pickle-serialized objects in agent memory storage. The vulnerability exists in the ConversationBufferMemory and VectorStoreRetrieverMemory components. Exploitation requires no authentication when the agent API is exposed. Over 15,000 publicly accessible LangChain deployments were identified via Shodan scanning. Patch available in LangChain 0.2.16. CISA added to KEV catalog on 2025-03-15.

title: Google DeepMind: Jailbreaking State-of-the-Art LLMs via Many-Shot In-Context Learning
text: We demonstrate that many-shot in-context learning can be exploited to systematically bypass safety guardrails in frontier LLMs. By prepending 100+ carefully crafted question-answer pairs to a harmful query, we achieve attack success rates of 85%+ against GPT-4, Claude 3, and Gemini Ultra. The attack scales with context window size — models with larger context windows are more vulnerable. We propose defenses including context length limits on safety-critical deployments and many-shot detection classifiers. The attack requires no model access beyond standard API queries.

title: Recorded Future: AI-Generated Deepfake CFO Impersonation Enables $47M Wire Fraud
text: Recorded Future Intelligence has documented a sophisticated business email compromise (BEC) campaign that used AI-generated real-time audio and video deepfakes to impersonate a Fortune 500 CFO in a video conference call. Employees authorized a $47M wire transfer after participating in what appeared to be a live video call with their CFO. The deepfake was generated using a voice clone trained on public earnings call recordings and a video model trained on investor presentation footage. This represents a significant escalation in AI-enabled financial fraud capabilities, moving from text-based BEC to live video impersonation.

title: OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications 2025: Updated Rankings and New Threats
text: OWASP has published the 2025 edition of the Top 10 for LLM Applications. Key changes from 2024: Prompt Injection retains #1 position with new indirect injection via tool outputs as a subtype. New entry: Agentic Risk (excessive agency, autonomous action without human oversight) rises to #3. RAG Poisoning elevated to #5. Supply Chain attacks on model weights and training data moved to #2. The report documents 150+ real-world incidents across the taxonomy. Recommended enterprise mitigations include LLM firewalls, output validation layers, and principle of least privilege for agent capabilities.

title: Anthropic Red Team Report: Systematic Evaluation of Claude 3.5 Against Agentic Misuse
text: Anthropic's red team conducted systematic evaluation of Claude 3.5 Sonnet in agentic configurations with computer use, browser access, and code execution capabilities. Key findings: Claude demonstrated appropriate refusal of 94% of direct harmful task requests. However, multi-step tasks using indirect framing achieved harmful outcomes in 23% of scenarios. The most effective attack vectors were task decomposition (splitting harmful goals into innocuous subtasks), gradual context manipulation, and role-playing as a security researcher. Anthropic implemented revised agentic guardrails and recommends staged rollout of computer use capabilities with human oversight checkpoints.

title: MITRE ATLAS Update: New Technique Categories for LLM and Agentic AI Attacks
text: MITRE ATLAS has added 23 new techniques and 8 new tactics to the AI adversarial tactics framework. New tactic added: LLM Agent Exploitation, covering indirect prompt injection, agent privilege escalation, and multi-agent compromise. New techniques under model supply chain: training data poisoning via synthetic data injection, model weight tampering via CI/CD pipeline compromise, and model card forgery. Coverage expanded to include agentic AI systems, RAG architectures, and multi-modal models. 47 new real-world case studies added from 2024 incidents. Framework now maps to NIST AI RMF for integrated risk management.

title: Unit 42: Chinese APT Group Using Custom LLM for Automated Vulnerability Research
text: Unit 42 has attributed a sophisticated AI-assisted vulnerability research and exploitation campaign to a Chinese APT group tracked as CarbonSpider. The group deployed a custom fine-tuned LLM trained on exploit code repositories, CVE databases, and reverse engineering datasets to automate vulnerability discovery in enterprise software. The LLM was used to analyze binary patches, generate proof-of-concept exploits, and prioritize targets by attack surface. The campaign resulted in zero-day exploitation of 4 enterprise VPN products before vendor disclosure. This represents the first confirmed case of a nation-state actor using a specialized LLM for offensive vulnerability research at scale.

title: NIST AI RMF 2.0 Draft: Expanded Guidance on Agentic AI Governance
text: NIST has released a draft update to the AI Risk Management Framework (AI RMF 2.0) with substantially expanded guidance on agentic AI systems. New sections cover: autonomous decision-making risk profiles, multi-agent system governance, human oversight requirements for high-stakes automated actions, and AI supply chain integrity. The draft introduces the concept of 'authority scope boundaries' for AI agents — defining what actions an agent is authorized to take without human confirmation. Public comment period open until May 2025. Organizations using agentic AI in critical processes are urged to review and submit comments.

title: Hugging Face Incident: Malicious Model Weights Containing Embedded Reverse Shell
text: Hugging Face Security disclosed that 12 model repositories on the platform were found to contain malicious code embedded in model weight files using the Pickle serialization format. When loaded via standard PyTorch or Transformers code, the models executed a reverse shell that connected to attacker-controlled infrastructure. The malicious models collectively received 34,000 downloads before removal. Affected models impersonated popular fine-tunes of Mistral, Llama-3, and Phi-3. Hugging Face has implemented mandatory malware scanning for all newly uploaded model files and is rolling out safe tensor format enforcement.

title: The Rise of AI-Powered Red Teaming Tools: Democratizing Adversarial Testing
text: A new generation of AI-powered red teaming and attack tools is lowering the skill barrier for offensive security operations. Tools including AutoAttack, PentestGPT, and HackBot now automate phases of the attack lifecycle including reconnaissance, vulnerability scanning, exploit generation, and lateral movement planning. Security researchers demonstrated that these tools reduce average time-to-exploit for known CVEs by 67%. Organizations are increasingly deploying AI-based defensive tools (AI SOC platforms, automated threat hunting) in response. The convergence is accelerating a defensive and offensive AI arms race, with concerns about tool proliferation enabling lower-skilled threat actors.

title: RAG Poisoning in Production: Threat Analysis of Retrieval-Augmented Generation Attacks
text: CrowdStrike Adversary Intelligence has analyzed a new attack surface emerging from enterprise deployments of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems. Adversaries can compromise RAG knowledge bases through document injection, metadata manipulation, and vector database tampering to cause the LLM to produce malicious outputs including confidential data disclosure, malicious code recommendations, and policy bypass. Three in-the-wild campaigns were documented where threat actors injected poisoned documents into shared knowledge bases in Microsoft SharePoint and Confluence to hijack enterprise LLM assistant responses. The attack is particularly effective because RAG outputs appear authoritative and contextually appropriate.

title: AI-Enabled Disinformation: State Actors Scale Synthetic Media Production for Elections
text: The Atlantic Council's Digital Forensic Research Lab has documented a significant escalation in state-sponsored AI-enabled disinformation campaigns targeting democratic elections across 8 countries in 2024-2025. Operations used AI text generation for scalable narrative seeding, synthetic video for false political statements, voice clones for audio misinformation, and AI-managed social media persona networks with realistic engagement patterns. Russia, China, and Iran-linked operations collectively produced an estimated 2.3 million pieces of synthetic media content during the monitored period. Detection rates by platform AI systems averaged only 41%, with audio deepfakes proving most difficult to detect.
